semanticCore: Rain-clouds, white water-carrying clouds, glowing moisture, or high status

keyDerivations.meaning: Rain-clouds, white moisture-bearing clouds, or clouds carrying rain

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَأَنْتُمْ أَنْزَلْتُمُوهُ مِنَ الْمُزْنِ أَمْ نَحْنُ الْمُنْزِلُونَ (56:69): Is it you who sent it down from the rain-clouds, or are We the senders?
summaryEn: The root refers to rain-bearing clouds (Muzn), serving as a rhetorical challenge about the divine origin of fresh drinking water.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى السحاب الأبيض الحامل للمطر والماء المنسكب العذب (مزن)، سائلاً البشر عن عجزهم وقدرة الله وحده في إنزال الماء من السحاب.
rawText: إِبْنُ مَازِنٍ The ant. (TA in art. بنى.) مُزْنٌ Clouds (K, and Ham, p. 564) of any kind: (Ham, ibid:) or white clouds: (S, K, and Ham, p. 53:) or clouds containing water. (K.)
